summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording captures a stellar set highlighted by Terrapin Station, Mississippi Half‑Step and an outstanding Wheel.
review: The show is repeatedly praised as one of the best in any collection – opening with a tight Bertha, soaring through Terrapin Station, Mississippi Half‑Step, Wharf Rat and a decade‑defining Wheel.  The audience (AUD) source is surprisingly clean and detailed, with vocals and instruments crystal‑clear despite the venue’s quirky acoustics; only the piano feels a touch heavy and one track (Do I Smell Sulfur) drags.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer scores (mostly 4‑5 stars), strong praise for both performance and audio clarity, with only minor criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very crisp and clear vocals/instruments; audience ambience audible at song starts/ends. Piano a bit heavy, one track (Do I Smell Sulfur) feels slow, but overall sound is superb.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Smooth flow from an energetic Bertha opener through a series of high‑energy jams; the second half builds with Terrapin Station and Mississippi Half‑Step, keeping momentum high throughout.

band_member_comments.Keys: Piano slightly heavy in the mix but otherwise solid.
